questions.name: Question 1
questions.question: Which of the following could be classified as discrete data?

answers.text: Number of students in a class

questions.name: Question 2
questions.question: Mario drives from his home to his friend's house. He watches a movie at his friend's house and then drives home. Which distance-time graph best represents Mario's complete journey? (Options A-D are the four graphs shown in the image.)

questions.name: Question 3
questions.question: Consider the network diagram shown. Which vertex has degree 4?

questions.name: Question 5
questions.question: A baker makes and sells cakes. The straight-line graphs represent cost (C) and revenue (R) in dollars, and n is the number of cakes, as shown. What profit will the baker make by selling 6 cakes?

questions.name: Question 6
questions.question: The network shows the distances, in kilometres, along a series of roads that connect towns, as shown (not to scale). What is the value of the largest weighted edge included in the minimum spanning tree for this network?

questions.name: Question 7
questions.question: A biased die is made from the net shown. The die is rolled once. What is the probability of rolling a 2?

questions.name: Question 8
questions.question: A spinner made up of 4 colours is spun 100 times. The frequency histogram shows the results. Which of these spinners is most likely to give the results shown? (Options A-D are the four spinners shown in the image.)

questions.name: Question 9
questions.question: An amount of $90 000 is invested at 4% per annum, compounded quarterly. Which expression gives the value of this investment, in dollars, after 6 years?

questions.name: Question 10
questions.question: The ratio of the dimensions of a model car to the dimensions of an actual car is 1:64. The actual car has a length of 4.9 m. What is the length of the model car in cm, correct to 1 decimal place?
